steps.title: Identify the grammatical clue
steps.content: The blank follows the adjective likely: “respondents were more likely ______ common Brazilian foods.” In Standard English, likely takes an infinitive (to + base verb) to express what someone is likely to do.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong forms and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Test each choice against the infinitive requirement:
  • Choice A — ate: simple past tense, not an infinitive — “more likely ate” is ungrammatical. ✗
  • Choice B — to eat: correct infinitive form — “more likely to eat” follows Standard English convention. ✓
  • Choice C — still be eating: progressive phrase missing the required to before it — ungrammatical after likely. ✗
  • Choice D — eaten: past participle, not an infinitive — “more likely eaten” is ungrammatical. ✗
That matches Choice B — to eat.
